pr.title: Fixup ftmaterial function call, cleanup itdrop
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Small merged cleanup/fix PR touching fighter material class initialization and item drop code. It changes ftMObj class initialization to use hsdMObj.parent as the parent class argument, and replaces three direct Item_GObj user_data accesses in itdrop.c with GET_ITEM after adding inlines.h.

key_files.role: Fixes ftMObj class initialization call argument from self parent to HSD MObj parent.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftmaterial.c hsdInitClassInfo call changes second argument from &ftMObj.parent to &hsdMObj.parent

key_files.role: Cleans item object data access by including inlines.h and using GET_ITEM for Item_GObj user_data retrieval.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_8026F3D4 changes item_gobj->user_data to GET_ITEM(item_gobj)
raw_diff: it_8026F6BC changes spawned_item_gobj->user_data to GET_ITEM(spawned_item_gobj)
raw_diff: it_8026F7C8 changes spawned_item_gobj->user_data to GET_ITEM(spawned_item_gobj)

what_changed.text: In ftMaterial_800BF260, the hsdInitClassInfo parent-class argument was changed from &ftMObj.parent to &hsdMObj.parent while keeping the destination class info as &ftMObj.parent.
itdrop.c now includes inlines.h, apparently to make GET_ITEM available.
Three direct item_gobj or spawned_item_gobj user_data reads were replaced with GET_ITEM calls in item drop functions.

smart_moves.text: The item cleanup is narrow: it only adds the include needed for the inline accessor and replaces the specific Item_GObj user_data reads, without changing surrounding item drop logic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itdrop.c hunks show only include and three accessor replacements

smart_moves.text: The ftmaterial fix is a one-argument correction in an existing initialization call, limiting blast radius.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a GObj is known to contain an Item, prefer the existing item accessor macro GET_ITEM over direct user_data access if local include support is available.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: replaced item_gobj->user_data and spawned_item_gobj->user_data with GET_ITEM(...)

decomp_lessons.lesson: For HSD class initialization wrappers, verify that the destination class info and parent class info arguments are distinct when initializing a derived class; in this PR, ftMObj initializes against hsdMObj.parent rather than ftMObj.parent.

naming_conventions.convention: No symbol renames are made. Existing address-style function names such as it_8026F3D4, it_8026F6BC, it_8026F7C8, and ftMaterial_800BF260 are preserved.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: function names unchanged in hunks

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use established inline/accessor macro cleanup instead of raw struct member access when it expresses the typed object payload.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: user_data accesses replaced with GET_ITEM(...)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Make the smallest possible call-argument correction for class initialization instead of introducing helper wrappers or broader refactors.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftmaterial.c only changes the second hsdInitClassInfo argument

searchable_terms: Fixup ftmaterial function call, cleanup itdrop

evidence_refs: raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for both changed files
notes: Evidence strongly identifies the exact changed files and line-level edits.
There is no PR body, review discussion, or explicit validation output in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer GET_ITEM for known Item_GObj payload access
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2610 replaced direct Item_GObj user_data reads in itdrop.c with GET_ITEM(...) and added inlines.h. Candidate lesson: when the object is known to be an item GObj, use the established item accessor rather than raw user_data access.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/it/itdrop.c include and three GET_ITEM replacements

accepted_candidate_records.title: Check parent class argument in HSD class initialization
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2610 changed ftMaterial_800BF260 so hsdInitClassInfo initializes ftMObj with &hsdMObj.parent as the parent argument instead of &ftMObj.parent. Candidate lesson: for derived HSD class setup, verify the parent class info argument against the intended base class.

source_update_candidates.title: Accessor macros for GObj payloads
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding an example under typed-fields/accessors noting that known GObj payloads should use established accessors such as GET_ITEM when local evidence supports the object type, rather than direct user_data reads.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itdrop.c replaces three direct user_data reads with GET_ITEM(...)
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides a compact example of accessor cleanup, but broader standard wording should be curator-reviewed before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: itdrop.c uses inlines.h for GET_ITEM
source_update_candidates.text: src/melee/it/itdrop.c added #include "inlines.h" in PR 2610 to support GET_ITEM use for Item_GObj payload access.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itdrop.c include hunk and GET_ITEM replacements
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible file-specific include/accessor fact; should be checked against current source before adding as a path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not claim this PR proves a successful match or regression-free cleanup; no verifier output is present.
Do not infer reviewer preferences; there were no reviews or comments in the slice.
Do not generalize every GObj user_data access to GET_ITEM; the evidence only covers known Item_GObj access in itdrop.c.
Do not infer broader HSD class hierarchy rules beyond the ftMObj/hsdMObj parent-argument correction shown in the diff.
